description: Add missing error-throw branches to issueCertificate so forbidden issuance conditions reject instead of resolving.

Return shapes and error codes must match the mutation-kill tests in certificate-issuance.mutation-kill.test.ts; the 6 'promise resolved instead of rejecting' cases all flip to reject after this lands. No test file may be modified. Additionally return the new cert id on success so the 'expected undefined to be new-cert-id' case passes.
data_model: certificates + certificate_templates + event_people + events in src/lib/db/schema — no schema changes; see .planning/data-requirements.md §18 for the Rules list.

description: Add single-retry-on-transaction-failure to issueCertificate so the retry mutation-kill test passes.
behavior: On transaction failure during certificate issuance, issueCertificate must retry exactly once with the same input. The second attempt opens a new db.transaction() and redoes the full insert + number-sequence lookup. If the retry also fails, the original error is re-thrown. The mutation-kill test (`expected +0 to be 2`) asserts txCallCount === 2. Implementation must not retry more than once and must not alter the persisted state on the first failure.

description: Enforce transaction write-order in activateCertificateTemplate: archive existing active of same type, then activate target.
behavior: activateCertificateTemplate must run inside a single db.transaction(). Inside the tx, it first UPDATEs any existing certificate_templates row matching (event_id, certificate_type, status='active') to status='archived', archivedAt=now(), updatedBy=<actor>. Only after that completes does it UPDATE the target template to status='active', updatedBy=<actor>. The mutation-kill tests assert this exact ordering via setCalls[0].status='archived' and setCalls[1].status='active'. Also assert mockDb.transaction was called. Data-requirements §17: 'One active template per (event_id, certificate_type) — prevents generation mistakes.'
data_model: certificate_templates — no schema changes. See data-requirements.md §17.

description: Confirm full Stryker baseline runs after cert code gaps close; produce per-module scores for 107 files.
behavior: After stories 1-4 land, run a full Stryker mutation-testing baseline with DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL_TEST npx tsx qa/controller.ts baseline. Stryker must successfully begin mutation analysis (vitest dry-run passes with 0 failures) and populate .quality/state.json plus commit a snapshot to qa/baselines/. ACCEPTABLE OUTCOMES (ladder, in order of preference): (a) PREFERRED — full baseline across all 107 files in the stryker.config.json mutate glob, exit code 0, all modules scored in state.json. (b) ACCEPTABLE — partial baseline covering >= 60 modules scored in state.json, with a plain-English note appended to the committed snapshot file explaining which modules were skipped and why (typically laptop OOM with exit 143/137). The partial is real data and still unblocks Block 2. (c) ABORT — if Stryker cannot complete even 60 modules, or if the dry-run fails, emit <promise>ABORT</promise>. Do NOT create shadow config files (stryker.conf.mjs or any variant), do NOT edit the locked stryker.config.json, and do NOT lower test expectations to force a completion. The lock is the contract; ABORT is the escape hatch. When an acceptable outcome is reached, commit the snapshot to qa/baselines/ and mark the story passes:true. This story writes no production code; it is a verification gate before Block 2 begins.
data_model: .quality/state.json — controller writes this automatically.

description: POST /api/events/[eventId]/certificates/bulk — Upstash-locked bulk generation with 409 on contention.
behavior: Body: { certificate_type, scope: 'all' | { ids: string[] } }. Before starting, acquire Redis lock 'lock:certificates:generate:{eventId}:{type}' with 5-minute TTL using SET NX. If NX fails, return 409 { error: 'generation in progress', lock_holder, started_at, expires_at } (read holder metadata from the lock value). On success, kick off the bulk run (sync or enqueued to Inngest — spec does not require sync). Release the lock on completion (both success and failure paths) via DEL, not TTL expiry. Per-cert atomicity: cert N failure leaves 1..N-1 issued and retryable; no rows remain in a non-terminal intermediate status. Return 202 { batch_id, total, lock_expires_at }.
data_model: issuedCertificates bulk-inserted; Upstash lock key. See BACKEND_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md Redis section.

description: Bulk generation is per-cert atomic; failure mid-batch leaves prior successes issued with no non-terminal rows.
behavior: Inside the bulk loop, each certificate is issued in its own transaction. On exception for cert N, log the failure, skip to the next cert, and continue. Do not abort the batch on a single failure. No row is ever left in a pending/intermediate status — rows are either status='issued' (success) or not present (not attempted or rolled back by its own tx). Failures are reported in a summary that can be retrieved via batch_id. Contract CE9 + CE20 enforce: 46 successes + 54 retryable absent rows when failure injected at cert 47/100.
data_model: issuedCertificates; no intermediate pending status.

description: notification_log has exactly one row per idempotency key; retries UPDATE in place.
behavior: On first send attempt, INSERT a notification_log row keyed by idempotency_key (unique constraint). On retry, UPDATE that same row: attempts++, last_error, lastErrorCode, sentAt (on success), status transitions (queued→sending→sent|failed). Never INSERT a second row for the same key. Attempts counter monotonically increases; never resets or decrements. Contract Invariants 3, 4 and CE4, CE18.

description: 3-attempt retry then dead-letter: Sentry + notification_log.failed + system_dispatch_failure red flag.
behavior: Inngest function wrappers use retries=3 with exponential backoff. On final failure (exhausted retries) or on non-retriable Zod validation error (retries=0): (a) set notification_log.status='failed' with last_error populated; (b) emit Sentry event kind='cascade-dispatch-failure' with { cascadeEvent, payload, attempts, channel, triggerId }; (c) upsert a red_flags row of flag_type='system_dispatch_failure' scoped to (eventId, targetEntityType, targetEntityId). No end-user notification is sent about the failure. Contract Invariants 9, 10, and CE15, CE16.
data_model: notification_log.status, red_flags (upsert by unique key).

unit.name: final-failure path emits all 3 signals
unit.description: Given 3 exhausted attempts, the dead-letter helper runs the log update, Sentry call, and red-flag upsert.
unit.expected_output: all three side-effects observed via spies

e2e.name: CE15 passes
e2e.expected: notification_log status=failed, attempts=3; sentry event present; red flag active

edge_cases.name: CE16 passes — no user-facing failure notification sent
steps: After dead-letter, probe notification_log for recipient=<user>

description: 5-second cascade debounce keyed on (eventId, sourceEntityType, sourceEntityId).
behavior: Deep module src/lib/cascade/debounce.ts wraps emitCascadeEvent. Before emitting, SETEX in Upstash with key 'cascade:debounce:{eventId}:{sourceEntityType}:{sourceEntityId}' and TTL 5s. If the key already exists, merge the new changes into the pending buffer (second key or hash) and do not emit a new Inngest event. When the debounce window expires (Inngest scheduled function or a worker-side timer), emit one event with the combined changeSummary. Different (sourceEntityType, sourceEntityId) tuples use distinct keys so they never coalesce. Contract Example 3, CE2, CE14.
data_model: No DB schema; Upstash Redis keys.

unit.name: second emit within 5s is swallowed
unit.description: Mock Redis; first emit sets key, second finds key present and skips Inngest.send.

unit.name: merged changeSummary
unit.description: Buffer concats change diffs; final emitted event contains both.

e2e.expected: one event; changeSummary has both field diffs

steps: Save two different travel records 2s apart

description: Variable snapshot captured at emit time, reused on retries.
behavior: Cascade event payload includes a variables snapshot: all template variable values resolved from current DB state at the moment of emit. Retry attempts reuse this snapshot verbatim; they never re-query the DB for fresh values. Implementation: the emitter resolves variables once and embeds them in payload.variables. The handler passes payload.variables to the template renderer. Contract CE7, CE20 and Invariant 5.
data_model: Payload only; no DB column.

unit.name: snapshot frozen into payload
unit.description: Emit captures variable values; subsequent DB edits to source entity do not change payload.variables.

description: Wire all 7 cascade events to their handlers per the frozen contract.

Each function is an independent Inngest handler with its own retry budget; failure in one does not affect siblings. Each handler filters every DB query by eventId (except people master reads).

description: Zod-validate cascade payload at handler entry; dead-letter on malformed without retry.
behavior: Each cascade handler's first step is a Zod parse on its expected payload shape. On parse failure, throw a NonRetriableError (or Inngest's NonRetriableError) so Inngest makes exactly one attempt and dead-letters. Emit a Sentry event kind='cascade-payload-invalid' with the raw payload and the Zod issues. No DB writes, no notification sends. Contract Example 9 and CE10.
data_model: No schema.

unit.name: missing eventId yields NonRetriableError

description: Red-flag creation is an upsert keyed on (event_id, target_entity_type, target_entity_id, flag_type).
behavior: Deep module src/lib/cascade/red-flags.ts exports upsertRedFlag(input). Uses ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE on a partial unique index covering the 4 key columns WHERE flag_status != 'resolved'. The resolved-state filter is important: a previously-resolved flag of the same tuple should allow a new active row. Handlers call this instead of raw insert. Contract Invariant 17 and CE8. Data-requirements §19 defines the active-flag uniqueness rule.
data_model: red_flags + partial unique index on (event_id, target_entity_type, target_entity_id, flag_type) WHERE flag_status != 'resolved'.

description: Every cascade handler query filters by cascade's eventId; no writes to other events.
behavior: Audit existing and new cascade handlers. Every .select() / .update() / .delete() / .insert() on an event-scoped table must include .where(eq(table.eventId, cascadeEvent.eventId)) or its transactional equivalent. The only exception is src/lib/db/schema/people — the master table is global. Add an eslint rule or AST-level lint (qa/diff-audit already has similar tooling) to reject merges that violate this. Contract CE9, CE19 and Invariant 13.

description: In-flight cascades complete after event transitions to archived.
behavior: When an event is archived, new mutations are blocked at the server-action level (assertEventAccess should reject writes on archived events). However, cascade events already emitted before the state transition should complete normally — handlers do not check event state on entry. Contract Example 10 and CE17: emit cascade at T=0, archive at T=1, handler runs at T=2 and sends the notification as if the event were still active. The archive restriction lives in mutation entry points, not in cascade handlers.
data_model: No schema change; behavior lives in auth/event-access + mutation routes.

description: Provider-mode shim in lib/notifications that toggles transports between normal/fail/failN/flaky and records sent bodies.
behavior: Intercept the Resend and Evolution API adapters. A state machine (backed by Upstash keys 'test:provider-mode:{channel}') decides whether to call the real transport or simulate a failure. Modes: 'normal' (real call), 'fail' (always throw), 'failN:<n>' (throw first n attempts then real call), 'flaky:<rate>' (random fail). The existing /api/test/provider-mode endpoint needs to be created to POST the mode. On every simulated or real send, persist the rendered body to 'test:last-sent:{triggerId}:{channel}' so /api/test/last-sent-body returns something. Fail modes throw the exact error shape the production code expects. Used by cascade-idempotency tests for retry / failure / flaky scenarios.
data_model: Upstash keys; no DB changes.

Merge must preserve referential integrity: event_people rows, travel/accommodation/session_assignments pointing at the merged id are re-pointed atomically inside a transaction. Actor and timestamp written to audit_log. .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md §People explicitly lists this as unbuilt. research-hub/PROJECT_HANDOFF.md M57 is the wireframe.

description: Send one aggregated email (and WhatsApp) per faculty member showing ALL their responsibilities across sessions.
behavior: New server action sendFacultyBundle(eventId) iterates distinct person_ids in session_assignments for the event, renders one React Email with the full responsibility list (sessions grouped by day, role per row, hall, timestamps in IST), and sends via the notification service using template key 'faculty_bundle'. WhatsApp variant is a condensed text version with a link to the public faculty confirmation page. Idempotency key: `notification:{userId}:{eventId}:faculty_bundle:{programVersionId}:{channel}`. Triggers: (a) on program.version_published cascade event; (b) manual 'Send Bundle' CTA on M26. Resend does NOT re-email already-acked faculty unless coordinator ticks 'Force resend'. Each recipient gets exactly one email per program version, not one per session. PRD §User Stories #24.

description: Revised-responsibility A/B/C diff notification on program version publish.
behavior: New cascade handler for conference/program.version_published computes a structured diff between the newly published program_version and the immediately prior published one. Per affected faculty, compute three buckets: A=added (new assignments), B=changed (role, hall, or time shifted), C=removed (assignment withdrawn). Render one email per faculty with the three buckets and send via the notification service. If a faculty member has zero net changes, skip. Idempotency key includes programVersionId and personId. PRD §User Story #56. This is distinct from the aggregated-bundle story: this fires on every republish and shows only diffs, not the full list.

description: Super Admin UI to assign Event Coordinators and Ops staff to specific events via event_user_assignments table.
behavior: User Story 5: 'As a Super Admin, I want to assign Event Coordinators and Ops staff to specific events, so that access is scoped per conference.' Currently event_user_assignments is only populated at event creation (creator auto-assigned as owner). There is no action or UI to assign additional team members to specific events. Super Admins see all events regardless, but Event Coordinators and Ops roles see only events they are explicitly assigned to — so without assignment, non-super-admin users can never access events except the ones they created. Needs: (a) assignUserToEvent(eventId, clerkUserId, assignmentType) server action, (b) removeUserFromEvent(eventId, clerkUserId) action, (c) listEventAssignments(eventId) action, (d) UI page or panel within event settings to manage event-level access. Source: .planning/prd.md §User Story 5; .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md §Events ('Per-event access control scopes...').

Remove button per row (with confirmation). Super Admin only.

description: CSV bulk import for travel records — Ops staff can upload a travel-agent CSV instead of entering records one-by-one.
behavior: User Story 46: 'As an Ops staff member, I want to import travel records and accommodation records from CSV, so that bulk data from travel agents and hotels can be loaded.' Travel records currently require manual entry via /events/[eventId]/travel/new. No bulk import path exists. Required: /events/[eventId]/travel/import route using react-spreadsheet-import (same pattern as /people/import) with column auto-mapping to travel_records fields. Lookup person by email or phone (E.164) against master people DB; reject rows with no match. Each imported row: INSERT travel_records scoped to eventId, upsert event_people, write audit_log, emit conference/travel.saved Inngest event. Duplicate guard: rows with same PNR for same person/event are skipped with a warning.
ui_details: Route /events/[eventId]/travel/import — 6-step react-spreadsheet-import flow. Required columns: person_identifier (email or phone), direction (inbound|outbound), mode (flight|train|bus|car), from_location, to_location, departure_at, arrival_at, pnr, carrier. Optional: terminal. Result screen shows imported/skipped/failed row counts with error detail per row.
data_model: travel_records INSERT (eventId, personId, direction, mode, fromLocation, toLocation, departureAtUtc, arrivalAtUtc, pnr, carrier, terminal); event_people upsert; audit_log row per imported record.

unit: Valid row with matching person email → travel_records row created scoped to eventId
Row with unknown email → skipped with 'person not found' error
Duplicate PNR for same person in same event → skipped with 'already exists' warning
eventId mismatch on any row → entire import rejected with 403
e2e: Upload CSV with 10 valid rows → 10 travel records created, cascade fires for each, audit_log has 10 entries
CSV with 3 unknown people → 3 skipped, 7 imported, result screen shows correct counts
edge_cases: Empty CSV → error: no rows to import
Column names don't match → auto-mapping UI prompts user to remap before import
Person exists in master DB but not yet in this event → event_people upserted automatically

description: CSV bulk import for accommodation records — Ops staff can upload a hotel rooming-list CSV instead of entering records one-by-one.
behavior: User Story 46 (accommodation half): Accommodation records currently require manual entry via /events/[eventId]/accommodation/new. No bulk import path exists. Required: /events/[eventId]/accommodation/import route using react-spreadsheet-import. Lookup person by email or phone; reject rows with no match. Enforce the existing travel-first rule: rows where the person has no travel_record for this event are skipped with a warning (consistent with the accommodation form's auto-filter). Each imported row: INSERT accommodation_records scoped to eventId, write audit_log, emit conference/accommodation.saved Inngest event. Shared_room_group populated if the CSV column is present — all occupants in the group receive a cascade flag on any subsequent change.

Result screen shows imported/skipped/failed counts with per-row error detail.
data_model: accommodation_records INSERT (eventId, personId, hotelName, address, city, roomType, roomNumber, checkInDate, checkOutDate, googleMapsUrl, sharedRoomGroup); event_people upsert; audit_log row per imported record.
